description: Code commits to the database then publishes to the broker as separate steps, so a crash between them loses or fabricates events.
body: Failure mode: the handler does db.commit() then broker.publish() (or the reverse); a crash, timeout, or broker error between the two leaves state and the event stream inconsistent. Detection signals: publish called after commit with no outbox row; events missing for committed changes or present for rolled-back ones; reconciliation drift over time. Do not patch with retry-after-commit, which can still double-publish or lose on crash. Safer fallback: write the event to an outbox table in the business transaction and relay it separately with idempotent consumers.
